description: VectorPlotter defines abstract methods (scale_native, scale_numeric, scale_datetime) that force subclasses to implement specific scaling logic.
reasoning: The VectorPlotter class acts as a base class that forces its subclasses to implement specific scaling logic (scale_native, scale_numeric, scale_datetime) instead of delegating this behavior to a dedicated Scale abstraction or strategy pattern. This forces subclasses to be aware of and implement low-level scaling details, violating the principle that abstractions should not depend on details.

description: Scale base class contains abstract methods that force specific implementation details on all scale types.
reasoning: The Scale class defines abstract methods like tick, label, and _get_locators. By forcing all subclasses to implement these specific interfaces, it creates a rigid contract where high-level scale abstractions are tightly coupled to the implementation details of Matplotlib's ticker system, rather than being truly decoupled abstractions.

description: Mark class forces subclasses to implement _plot and _legend_artist, tying them to specific drawing implementations.
reasoning: The Mark class forces subclasses to implement _plot and _legend_artist. This forces concrete mark types (Line, Bar, etc.) to handle the low-level rendering logic and Matplotlib integration directly, rather than depending on a higher-level rendering abstraction.
